runnable_after_repro_check.note: auto entry-point discovery + known-pattern source patches + opt-in dependency install

headline: 3/12 (25%) of these repos run as-cloned under a 2026 environment; repro-check raised that to 4/12 (33%) automatically, and produced a diagnosed next-step for every remaining repo.
boundaries_confirmed: erfit (ERpy): pip install was ATTEMPTED and FAILED (package not on PyPI); the tool then handed off to the agent. Outcome is correct, but v2 had no pre-check for local/unpublished modules — the same path would fire for a typo'd or lookalike name. See v3 fix below.
no-progress + no-op guards stop ineffective-patch loops (ramp-metering fixed) — real logic
dependency install advances repos to their NEXT real blocker (honest partial progress)
next_patterns_to_add: IMPORT_NAME_MISMATCH (pyDOE code importing pyDOE after pyDOE2 install)

advanced_not_run: ten-year-challenge-paper-3: py2 syntax fixed -> now fails on a real ValueError in the computation (honest deeper failure, hands off)
v2_update.known_fragility: PY2_SYNTAX uses lib2to3 (deprecated; cannot parse 3.10+ syntax). Adequate for old py2 code but flag for replacement.

saturation_question.method: count repos that force a NEW failure pattern into the library; watch the rate flatten
saturation_question.honest_caveat: The tail-flattening in the curve is NOT evidence of saturation — the discovery order was reconstructed post-hoc and the low-novelty repos happen to sit last. With n=12 and 67% still teaching something, the only defensible conclusion is that we are clearly PRE-saturation. A real saturation estimate needs repos processed in arrival order with the novelty rate tracked prospectively over a larger n (target ~40-60).
saturation_question.conclusion: Not near diminishing returns. Continue expanding the corpus.

v3_update.fix: Added looks_local() pre-check: a missing-import name that resolves to a .py file or package dir inside the repo is REFUSED for install before pip is called.
v3_update.why: v2 attempted `pip install <name>` for any missing module and only handed off after pip failed — so 'correctly refused erfit' described the outcome, not real logic, and the same path would fire for a typo'd or malicious lookalike name.
v3_update.verified: ERpy: erfit detected as local package dir -> NEEDS_AGENT with reason 'local module ... install refused', installed=[] (no pip attempt).

full_corpus_v4.corpus: ReScience-C Python, 45 repos (43 evaluable, 2 dead clone links), arrival order
full_corpus_v4.after_current_fixes: 16/43 (37%) [yaml Loader, dep install, api-rename, path repoint]
full_corpus_v4.reachable_with_this_session_fixes: 23/43 (~53.5%) [+OpenMP KMP_DUPLICATE_LIB_OK (6), +run-as-module (1); the 2 numpy-attr repos are dependency-internal rot, NOT source-fixable]. NOTE: an earlier v4 draft said 25/43 (58%) by counting those 2 numpy repos as fixable; corrected in v6_close_the_loop. This is an internal 'reachable' metric (crash cleared / advanced), separate from the public runs-to-completion figure of 16/43 (37%).
full_corpus_v4.genuine_agent_handoff: 5/19 of the reclassified tail are strictly bespoke; corpus-wide the agent-needed set is ~13-16/43 once main-run DEP/PATH failures and the 3 mixed cases are included.
full_corpus_v4.not_broken_timeouts: 2/43 (heavy real computation, needs bigger cap not a fix)
full_corpus_v4.key_correction: The '63% bespoke tail' from v3 was WRONG. Capturing tracebacks showed that of the 19 UNKNOWN repos: 9 are recurring library-fixable patterns (dominated by ONE OpenMP crash, 6 repos, that a single env var neutralises), 5 are genuine agent hand-offs (CLI-args 2, shape ValueError 1, pandas/data 1, truly-bespoke 1), 3 are mixed/case-by-case, and 2 are timeouts (heavy runs, not broken). So 5 bespoke — at most 8 if the 3 mixed also need the agent — not the 63% implied before.
full_corpus_v4.openmp_fix_verified: SimCLR: rc=-6 (OpenMP abort) WITHOUT -> clears WITH KMP_DUPLICATE_LIB_OK=TRUE, advances to dataset download (data availability, not code fault).
full_corpus_v4.revised_saturation_read: Novelty did NOT truly saturate at ~11 repos — it only appeared to because the coarse classifier dumped everything into UNKNOWN. A finer classifier keeps finding teachable recurring patterns. Diminishing returns is FURTHER out than v3 implied.
full_corpus_v4.library_vs_agent_boundary: ~53.5% reachable by a fixed library (env + rot + structure; the 2 numpy dependency-internal repos are NOT source-fixable, correcting the earlier ~58%); the rest genuinely needs the agent to read a traceback and reason. Public runs-to-completion figure is 16/43 (37%). This quantifies the seam.

added_patterns: NDARRAY_METHOD: a.ptp() -> np.ptp(a) (numpy 2.0 removal); restricted to ptp only after audit — itemset/newbyteorder have no top-level np equivalent
v6_close_the_loop.key_finding_dependency_internal_rot: The 2 'numpy ndarray.ptp' failures are NOT in the repos' own code — grep found no .ptp( in either source tree. The error is raised INSIDE a dependency (Brian2's fundamentalunits.py does `ptp = wrap_function_keep_dimensions(np.ndarray.ptp)` at import under numpy 2.0). So source-patching the repo cannot fix it; the real fix is pinning/upgrading the dependency or patching site-packages — squarely an AGENT/environment job, not a source-rewrite.
v6_close_the_loop.reclassification_correction: These 2 repos were counted 'library-fixable (numpy-rot)' in v4. That was optimistic — they are dependency-internal rot, which the source-patch engine CANNOT fix. Corrected split of the 9 'library-fixable': 6 OpenMP (verified via env var), 1 run-as-module (verified), 2 dependency-internal-rot (NOT source-fixable -> agent/env). So verified-library-fixable is 7, not 9. Reachable = 16 (prior fixes) + 6 (OpenMP) + 1 (run-as-module) + 0 (ptp) = 23/43 (~53.5%), down from the optimistic v4 ~58%.
v6_close_the_loop.lesson: 'AttributeError from numpy' is ambiguous until you see WHERE it's raised. Rot in the repo's own code is source-fixable; identical rot inside a pinned dependency is not. The engine must distinguish traceback origin (repo vs site-packages) before claiming a fix.

patterns_added_and_verified.OPENMP (env var): VERIFIED fixes 6 repos — SimCLR rc=-6 abort -> clears, advances to data download
patterns_added_and_verified.NDARRAY_METHOD a.ptp()->np.ptp(a): IMPLEMENTED + audited to ptp-only; but the 2 corpus cases turned out to be DEPENDENCY-INTERNAL (Brian2), so source-patch cannot help them
patterns_added_and_verified.RELATIVE_IMPORT (run as -m): VERIFIED on gabni — relative-import error cleared; advanced to a --input CLI-arg requirement (correct hand-off)

v7_loop_closed.honest_outcome: Of the 3 'library-fixable' classes I set out to close: OpenMP clears the crash for 6 repos; run-as-module genuinely clears gabni's blocker (then a CLI-arg wall); the numpy-ptp pair are NOT source-fixable (dependency-internal). Net verified reachable = 23/43 (~53.5%), below the optimistic v4 projection of ~58%.

revised_reachable_estimate.plus_prior_fixes: 16/43 (37%)  [yaml Loader, dep install, api-rename, path repoint]
revised_reachable_estimate.crash_cleared_vs_runs_to_completion: 23/43 is 'crash cleared / advanced past a plumbing wall'. FULLY runs-to-completion is LOWER: several of the +6/+1 advance only to a data-download or CLI-arg wall that is a genuine agent/human step (gabni needed --input + data supplied by hand).

v7_loop_closed.clean_finding: Closing the loop confirmed the architecture: plumbing fixes (env, structure) are cheap and reliable; the moment a fix lands, the NEXT blocker is disproportionately a genuine agent/human step (CLI args, missing data, in-code ValueError). The library clears the runway; the agent flies the plane.
